sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the output, the top-right square (id=3) is now G (collected)! And the white block (id=4) is at (29.5, 20.0) with 4px. So pressing SPACE at the top-right square collected it (turned G) without needing to pick it up first.
Wait, but earlier I thought SPACE toggles pick/drop. Let me reconsider:
- First SPACE at top-right: picked it up (white grew to 16px)
- Moved to left square
- Second SPACE at left: dropped the top-right square and picked up the left square? Or just collected the left square?
- Third SPACE at bottom: picked up bottom square
- Fourth SPACE: dropped bottom square (turned G)
- Fifth SPACE at top-right: collected it (turned G)
Hmm, the pattern isn't consistent. Let me look at what changed:
- Before: top-right square was c at (29.5, 17.5)
- After: top-right square is G at (29.5, 17.5), white block is at (29.5, 20.0) with 4px
So the top-right square is now G. The white block is 4px (not carrying anything). The left square is still c at (21.5, 17.5).
Now I need to collect the left square. Let me go there and press SPACE.
Current player position: green at (29.5, 22.0), white at (29.5, 20.0)
Left square at (21.5, 17.5)
Need to go LEFT and UP.
- LEFT: col 22 -> 17.5 ≈ 1 move
- UP: row 29.5 -> 21.5 ≈ 2 moves

# Go to left square at (21.5, 17.5) from player at (29.5, 22.0)
# LEFT: 22 -> 17.5 ≈ 1 move
# UP: 29.5 -> 21.5 ≈ 2 moves
action(['LEFT', 'UP', 'UP'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
